assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable gives rise to a foreign-currency-denominated balance or transaction exposure.
The question concerns the accounting in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency translation accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting for exchange differences on monetary items, including the consolidation treatment of intragroup monetary balances that are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for eligible hedged items and hedging relationships.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats cash flow hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model, including for eligible highly probable forecast transactions and foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9, IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 set out a distinct hedge accounting model for hedges of foreign currency risk arising from net investments in foreign operations.
